name: react-error-boundary-expert
description: Architectural opinions for React error handling strategies in enterprise applications

error_boundary_granularity.title: Error Boundary Granularity: How Many Levels?
error_boundary_granularity.description: Determine the appropriate number and placement of error boundary levels
error_boundary_granularity.rationale: More error boundaries provide better error isolation but add complexity. The right balance depends on app structure and failure criticality.

options.name: 4-Level Hierarchy (Recommended for Enterprise)
options.description: Global → Layout → Page → Component error boundaries
options.use_when: Complex dashboard apps, mission-critical apps, customer-facing products

options.name: 2-Level Hierarchy (Minimum Viable)
options.use_when: Internal tools, simple apps, MVP phase

decision_factors: Number of independent widgets per page

retry_strategy.title: Automatic vs Manual Retry
retry_strategy.description: Should errors automatically retry or require user action?
retry_strategy.rationale: Automatic retries reduce user friction for transient errors but can cause infinite loops or mask permanent errors. Manual retries give users control but require more interaction.

options.name: Hybrid (Recommended)
options.description: Automatic retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts), then manual retry button
pros: Recovers from brief network glitches automatically
User doesn't see error for transient issues
options.use_when: Production enterprise applications with network dependencies

options.name: Manual Only
options.description: Show error immediately with retry button
cons: User sees errors for brief network glitches
options.use_when: Internal tools where users understand network issues

options.name: Automatic Only
cons: Poor UX if retries take long time

decision_factors: Error transience (how often are errors temporary?)

error_notification_severity.title: Error Notification Strategy
error_notification_severity.description: How to notify users of errors based on severity
error_notification_severity.rationale: Critical errors need prominent UI (modal), medium errors need noticeable feedback (toast), low errors can be inline. Matching notification to severity improves UX.

context.when_not_applicable: Never - always notify users of errors

options.name: Severity-Based Notifications (Recommended)
options.description: Critical → Modal, High → Persistent Toast, Medium → Toast, Low → Inline
pros: Appropriate urgency for each error type

options.name: Toast Only
options.description: All errors show as toast notifications
options.use_when: Internal tools where all errors are medium severity

decision_factors: Error severity distribution in your app
